affordances.name: Recovery path check
affordances.mechanism: The model forces a decision to identify which downstream dependency becomes harder to reverse after the current move.

use_when: The answer celebrates a local gain while delayed dependencies may remove a cheap recovery path.
do_not_use_when: The decision is reversible and has no delayed dependency chain.
case_evidence_needed: a current move and a later dependency whose reversibility could change

treatment_requirements.description: Name the later dependency that becomes harder to reverse because the current move happens now.
evidence_required: first signal that reversal cost has changed
good_output_shape: This move removes the cheap recovery path at milestone X.

diagnostic_questions: Which later dependency becomes harder, costlier, or politically harder to reverse because this move happens now?
misuse_guards: Do not use second-order language to postpone commitment without naming an action threshold.

review_notes.normalization_note: Intentional invalid fixture: the affordance has no source evidence.
